overview.brief_summary: A targeted spearphishing campaign against transportation system employees leads to initial access, followed by execution of malicious code injection and defacement, ultimately resulting in automated exfiltration of sensitive data.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker sends a targeted phishing email to transportation system employees, tricking them into visiting a malicious link or downloading malware.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: A compromised website is visited by a transportation system employee on their company device, leading to drive-by download of malware.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Malicious code is injected into transportation system software, allowing the attacker to execute arbitrary commands.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attacker uses automated tools to exfiltrate sensitive data from the transportation system database.

Scenario.summary: Operation Sharpshooter is a targeted spearphishing campaign aimed at gaining initial access to transportation system employees, followed by execution of malicious code injection and defacement, ultimately resulting in automated exfiltration of sensitive data.
